01_initial_request.input_data: Protein sequence-structure-dynamics information and structural information for Protein-Protein Interaction (PPI) graph
01_initial_request.user_intent: To develop a novel deep neural network to accurately predict inter-protein PTM cross-talk by integrating heterogeneous structural-dynamics information with PPI graph contextual information, overcoming limitations of existing methods and experimental costs
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Achieve a prediction performance with an AUC value of 0.869, outperforming existing state-of-the-art methods

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PTM cross-talk research requires experimentally verified data pairs. [Gap] No comprehensive dataset of inter-protein PTM cross-talk pairs existed. [Decision] To achieve data collection goal, chose to use PubMed database search to perform literature mining on PTM cross-talk pairs, expecting to obtain experimentally verified cross-talk pairs.

parameters.keyword_combination: ((cross AND (talk OR regulate OR link)) OR interplay) AND post translational modification

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained 37 cross-talk pairs in interproteins from 735 associated articles

references.relevance: Followed the steps proposed by Huang et al. to collect experimentally verified PTM cross-talk pairs

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Historical PTM cross-talk data before 2017 exists in literature. [Gap] Need to incorporate previously collected data for comprehensive dataset. [Decision] To achieve complete data collection, chose to use Huang et al. dataset to perform integration of historical cross-talk pairs, expecting to obtain 199 additional cross-talk pairs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 199 cross-talk pairs before 2017 collected by Huang et al. were integrated

references.relevance: Previously collected PTM cross-talk pairs used in this work

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein sequences are needed for feature extraction. [Gap] Sequences for the 104 human proteins in cross-talk dataset not yet obtained. [Decision] To achieve sequence data collection, chose to use UniProt database to perform protein sequence download, expecting to obtain sequences for 104 human proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Downloaded protein sequences of the 104 human proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PTM site information is required for cross-talk prediction. [Gap] PTM sites for the 104 proteins need to be identified. [Decision] To achieve PTM site collection, chose to use PhosphoSitePlus database to perform PTM site search, expecting to obtain all PTM sites for the proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Searched all PTM sites from the PhosphoSitePlus database

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-quality protein structures are needed for structural feature extraction. [Gap] 3D structures for the 104 proteins need to be obtained. [Decision] To achieve structure data collection, chose to use AlphaFold database to perform PDB file download with quality filtering, expecting to obtain high-confidence protein structures.

parameters.filtering_criteria: residues with AlphaFold confidence score (pLDDT) less than 50 were removed

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Downloaded PDB files of the 104 human proteins with residues having pLDDT < 50 removed

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PPI information is crucial for capturing long-range dependencies. [Gap] PPI data between the 104 proteins needs to be collected. [Decision] To achieve PPI data collection, chose to use multiple PPI databases (STRING, HPRD, I2D, BioGrid) to perform PPI information extraction, expecting to obtain comprehensive PPI network.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained 852 interacting edges between 104 proteins (852 pairs of PPIs)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Evolutionary information between protein pairs is important for PTM cross-talk prediction. [Gap] Evolutionary features need to be calculated for PTM sites. [Decision] To achieve evolutionary feature extraction, chose to use Evol in ProDy to perform co-evolution calculation, expecting to obtain evolutionary information between protein pairs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calculated evolutionary information between protein pairs

references.relevance: Tool for bridging protein sequence evolution and structural dynamics

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Structural network topology features are important for PTM cross-talk characterization. [Gap] Network features based on 3D structures need to be calculated. [Decision] To achieve structural feature extraction, chose to use NACEN to perform network feature calculation, expecting to obtain structure-based network features.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calculated network features based on the 3D structures of proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein dynamics features are crucial for capturing PTM cross-talk potential. [Gap] Dynamic features need to be calculated for PTM sites. [Decision] To achieve dynamics feature extraction, chose to use ENMs in ProDy to perform dynamic feature calculation, expecting to obtain dynamics features for PTM cross-talk residues.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calculated a series of dynamic features with feature vector (32) representing each residue underlying PTM cross-talk

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Pre-W net requires preprocessing of extracted features. [Gap] Features need to be preprocessed to obtain W weight vector. [Decision] To achieve feature preprocessing, chose to use Pre-W net to perform feature vector preprocessing, expecting to obtain W weight vector (Fw).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained preprocessed W weight vector (Fw) from sequence, structure and dynamics features

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein sequence features need to be encoded for PPIG net. [Gap] Protein sequence feature vectors need to be extracted. [Decision] To achieve sequence encoding, chose to use FEGS to perform protein sequence feature extraction, expecting to obtain 578-dimensional feature vector for each protein.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained 578-dimensional vector fp as the feature vector for each protein

references.relevance: Feature Extraction based on Graphical and Statistical features method

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PPI graph needs to be encoded to capture structural information. [Gap] PPI graph embedding needs to be performed. [Decision] To achieve PPI graph encoding, chose to use SDNE to perform graph embedding, expecting to obtain encoded PPI graph features.

parameters.captures: local and global structure of the network

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Encoded the PPI graph capturing both local and global structure while preserving network information

references.citation: Wang D, Cui P, Zhu W [56]

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PTM cross-talk graph needs to be encoded separately from PPI graph. [Gap] Cross-talk graph embedding needs to be performed. [Decision] To achieve cross-talk graph encoding, chose to use Linear_VAE to perform graph embedding, expecting to obtain encoded cross-talk graph features.

parameters.edge_weight: number of cross-talks between protein pairs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Encoded the PTM cross-talk graph to extract corresponding node feature (Fp) and combination feature of protein pair

references.relevance: Graph autoencoders without graph convolutional networks

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] HRF net needs to combine heterogeneous features from Pre-W and PPIG nets. [Gap] Features need to be combined for final prediction. [Decision] To achieve feature combination, chose to use HRF net with bilinear feature module to perform heterogeneous feature fusion, expecting to obtain combined features for PTM cross-talk prediction.

parameters.reconstruction: flattened layer to one-dimensional tensor fC

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Combined Fw and Fpair features using bilinear feature module for final prediction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Final prediction model needs to be trained on combined features. [Gap] Classification model needs to be selected and trained. [Decision] To achieve PTM cross-talk prediction, chose to use Random Forest to perform classification training, expecting to obtain prediction scores for PTM cross-talk.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: RF outperformed other methods with AUC 0.869 and AUPR 0.852

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model performance needs to be evaluated with proper cross-validation. [Gap] Evaluation metrics need to be calculated. [Decision] To achieve model evaluation, chose to use 10-fold cross-validation to perform performance assessment, expecting to obtain comprehensive evaluation metrics.

parameters.sampling_ratio: one-to-one ratio for positive and negative samples

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Obtained evaluation indicators: AUC, Sn, Sp, Pre, ACC, F1 and MCC

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model performance needs to be compared with existing state-of-the-art methods. [Gap] Comparison with PTM-X method needed. [Decision] To achieve performance comparison, chose to use PTM-X as baseline to perform face-to-face comparison, expecting to demonstrate PPICT superiority.

parameters.comparison_method: Random Forest with coevolution and co-modification features

02_agent_trajectory.observation: PPICT significantly improved prediction performance compared with PTM-X (AUC: 0.869 vs 0.774)

references.citation: Li Y, Huang Y, Li T [19]
references.relevance: PTM-X prediction method for PTM cross-talk

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Functional validation of predicted PTM cross-talks is needed. [Gap] Biological relevance of predictions needs to be assessed. [Decision] To achieve functional validation, chose to use DAVID Bioinformatics Resources to perform GO and KEGG pathway enrichment analysis, expecting to obtain enriched biological pathways.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified enriched pathways including 'Prostate cancer', 'Pathways in cancer', 'Hepatitis B' and transcription-related functions

references.relevance: DAVID web server for functional enrichment analysis
DAVID bioinformatics resources for systematic analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model needs validation on potential PTM cross-talks in enzyme-substrate systems. [Gap] Predictability for functional PTM cross-talks needs verification. [Decision] To achieve model validation, chose to use kinase-, HAT-, E3-substrate datasets to perform score ranking analysis, expecting to validate high prediction performance on potential cross-talks.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: PPICT ranked PTM pairs involved in kinase-, HAT- and E3-substrate higher than control sets; almost all kinase-substrate cross-talks scored greater than 0.5

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ablation studies are needed to evaluate contribution of different features. [Gap] Individual component contributions need assessment. [Decision] To achieve ablation analysis, chose to use W_PPI, W_CT, PPICT*, PPICT_Seq variants to perform component evaluation, expecting to quantify feature contributions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Full PPICT model (AUC: 0.869) outperformed all ablation variants, demonstrating complementary value of PPI graph and cross-talk graph information

03_success_verification.validation_technique: 10-fold cross-validation with comprehensive metrics evaluation and comparison with state-of-the-art method PTM-X

AUC.interpretation: Area under the ROC curve, significantly outperformed PTM-X (0.774) and met the quantifiable goal

AUPR.interpretation: Area under the precision-recall curve, improved from PTM-X (0.785)

Sensitivity.interpretation: True positive rate for PTM cross-talk prediction

Specificity.interpretation: True negative rate for PTM cross-talk prediction

F1_score.interpretation: Harmonic mean of precision and recall

MCC.interpretation: Matthew's correlation coefficient for balanced evaluation

03_success_verification.final_verdict: SUCCESS - The research objective was achieved. PPICT achieved an AUC value of 0.869, outperforming the existing state-of-the-art method PTM-X (AUC: 0.774). The model successfully integrated protein sequence-structure-dynamics information with PPI graph contextual information, overcoming limitations of existing methods. Validation on kinase-, HAT-, and E3-substrate datasets confirmed high prediction performance on potential PTM cross-talks.
